workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Samantha Okafor at Evergreen Solutions just sent this. Help me run the hygiene check.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 14:02 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-02-03 10:54 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-28 17:27 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Evergreen Solutions: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-C trap (Invents prior commitments not in thread); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-C failure mode (Invents prior commitments not in thread)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-C -- Invents prior commitments not in thread
